title: Euphorbia tessellata
taxonomy: Euphorbia tessellata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family includes around 300 genera and over 7,500 species, commonly known as the spurge family. Euphorbia tessellata is a succulent plant, belonging to the genus Euphorbia, which contains approximately 2,000 species. The plant is native to Madagascar, a country with a diverse range of flora and fauna. The classification of Euphorbia tessellata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Euphorbia tessellata has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. The plant's milky sap is also used to treat wounds and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses. In some cases, it is also used to treat digestive issues and other ailments.

constituents: Euphorbia tessellata contains various active constituents, but specific information is limited.
